Zero errors. Here's a precise breakdown of every problem found and what was fixed:
Root cause 1 — Wrong shader (Lit instead of Unlit)

The existing tile materials (`Mat_Open.mat`, `Mat_Deepspace.mat`, etc.) use the URP Lit shader. `PlanetManager` was creating new materials with `Shader.Find("Universal Render Pipeline/Unlit")`, which is a different shader that doesn't exist on those tiles — so the runtime-created material was technically correct, but when assigned, URP would still render it subject to whatever render features are active. More importantly, `Color.cyan` / `Color.red` / `Color.yellow` are only linear-space values o

`HexRenderer.Start()` creates the `Hex_x_y` child GameObjects. `PlanetManager.Start()` called `PlacePlanets()` — and Unity's `Start` execution order between two unrelated GameObjects is not guaranteed. If `PlanetManager.Start` fired first, `hexGrid.transform.childCount` was 0, every `Find()` failed silently with just a warning, and no materials were ever assigned. Fixed by routing through a one-frame coroutine (`PlacePlanetsNextFrame`) so `HexRenderer` always finishes its `Start` before planet v

What changed
* Added `using System.Collections;` for `IEnumerator`.
* Added `PlayerColor`, `AIColor`, `NeutralColor` static readonly fields with fully saturated values (neutral is `(1, 0.85, 0)` rather than pure `(1,1,0)` which avoids the slightly green tint in linear color space).
* `Start()` now launches `PlacePlanetsNextFrame()`coroutine instead of calling `PlacePlanets()` directly.

* `VisualizePlanet()` logs the child name, owner, shader name, and resolved color for every planet it processes, plus a warning with `childCount` if the child isn't found.
* `BuildOwnerMaterials()` logs which shader it resolved and falls back gracefully.
* `CreateOwnerMaterial()` sets both `_BaseColor` and`_Color` (not `else if`) so it works against either shader variant, and explicitly sets `_Surface = 0` (Opaque) to prevent any transparency artefacts.

   * `EndTurnButton` — bottom-right, `onClick` wired directly to `GalacticTurnManager.EndTurn()` as a persistent call

* `BattleSceneSetup.cs` — if `CombatTransition.PendingEncounter` is set, spawn those ships instead of the single hardcoded definition.

The plan is ready. Here's a summary of what I found and what the plan specifies:

* `CombatTransition.cs` — polls `Keyboard.bKey` every `Update()` and calls `SceneLoader.LoadSceneAsync("Battle")`. No data handoff whatsoever.
* `UnitManager.MoveSelectedShip` — blocks moves onto anyoccupied hex, regardless of team. No post-move hook.
* `BattleSceneSetup` — spawns one hardcoded `playerShipDefinition` then calls `TestEnemySpawner` via `OnBattleSceneReady`. No encounter data awareness.

* `VictoryConditionChecker` — returns to GameMap after 3 s, but never cleans up the galactic map state.
* `EncounterData` — already declared in `GalacticTurnManager.cs` with `hexPosition`, `playerShips`, `enemyShips`. Reused directly.

`TestShipSpawner.cs`
* Line 32 — `UnitManager.Instance.SpawnShip(shipToSpawn, spawnPosition)` → spawns player ship (no team arg, defaults to `ShipTeam.Player`)

* Line 38 — `UnitManager.Instance.SpawnShip(s...ector2Int(7, 7), ShipTeam.Enemy)` → spawns enemy ship
`BattleSceneSetup.cs`
* Line 78 — `UnitManager.Instance.SpawnShip(p...rSpawnPosition, ShipTeam.Player)` → fallback player spawn
* Line 115 — `UnitManager.Instance.SpawnShip(s...playerSlots[i], ShipTeam.Player)` → loop spawning player fleet from `PendingEncounter`
* Line 129 — `UnitManager.Instance.SpawnShip(s..., enemySlots[i], ShipTeam.Enemy)` → loop spawning enemy fleet from `PendingEncounter`

The likely source of the extra player ship is `TestShipSpawner.cs`line 32 calling the no-team overload, which routes to `SpawnShip(definition, gridPosition, ShipTeam.Player)` at `UnitManager.cs` line 82. That's the only place a player ship is spawned outside of `BattleSceneSetup`. If `BattleSceneSetup` is also running (e.g. if the GameMap scene somehow triggers it, or if `PendingEncounter` is populated), lines 78 and 115 could double-spawn player ships too.

still two greens tho?
The only change is iterating `shipVisuals.Values`, calling `Destroy` on each non-null visual, then calling `shipVisuals.Clear()` before the existing three list/dict clears.

On the color question — `SpawnEncounterShips` is already correct. Tracing the full path:
* Line 129: `SpawnShip(src.definition, enemySlots[i], ShipTeam.Enemy)` → passes `ShipTeam.Enemy`
* `SpawnShip` line 118: `ApplyTeamColor(visual, team)` → `team` is `ShipTeam.Enemy`
* `ApplyTeamColor` line 321: `Color color = team == ShipTeam.P...PlayerShipColor : EnemyShipColor` → resolves to `EnemyShipColor` = `(1, 0, 0, 1)` ✓

If you're seeing enemy ships appear green in-game, the most likely cause is that `ClearAllShips` wasn't destroying old visuals — so a stale green player-ship clone from the GameMap scene was persisting into the Battle scene on top of the newly spawned red enemy. The fix above should resolve that symptom.

Let me inspect everything in parallel.
I found the root cause. There are two critical problems:
1. No EventSystem in the scene. The hierarchy shows every GameObject — there is no `EventSystem`. Without it, no UI click events are dispatched at all, making every button dead.

2. `TurnCounterText` has `raycastTarget: true` on its `TextMeshProUGUI`. While it's small and positioned top-left, the real blocker is the missing EventSystem.
Additionally, `TechTreeUIController.Start()` guard-returns early because `openButton`is a `Button` reference pointing to `TechTreePanel` being inactive at `Start()` time — but since `openButton` references `TechTreeButton` (which IS active), that's fine. The `AddListener` backup is still worth adding.

Let me read the current code precisely before answering.
Here's the exact logic flow for each question.

1. No ship selected, clicking a hex that has both a ship AND a planet
The ship wins — always. `GetShipAt()` is checked first (line 352). If it returns non-null, the method immediately selects that ship and shows the ship tooltip. `GetPlanet()` is never called. There is no "ship on a planet" compound case — only the ship branch executes.
2. Ship IS selected, clicking a different hex — does it check for another ship first?

No. With a ship selected, the entire `if (selectedShip == null)` branch is skipped. It goes straight to `MoveSelectedShip(cell.Coordinates)` (line 375) regardless of what's at the destination. The move check for an occupant does happen inside `MoveSelectedShip` at line 265-273 — but only to block friendly-on-friendly stacking or trigger combat. It does not let you switch selection to a different friendly ship. Clicking your own Frigate while another is selected will log `"Cannot move ship: targe
3. Can you deselect by clicking an empty hex?

No. Clicking an empty hex while a ship is selected calls `MoveSelectedShip` on that empty hex. Since `GetShipAt` returns null there (no occupant), `MoveSelectedShip` proceeds, moves the ship to that empty hex, and clears selection. There is no deselect-without-moving path. An empty-hex click is always interpreted as a move order.
4. Any right-click handling for movement vs left-click for selection?

None whatsoever. `HexSelector.OnHexSelected` fires on a single input event with no button discrimination — `HandleHexSelected` receives only a `HexCell` and has no awareness of which mouse button was pressed. All four interactions (select, move, planet tooltip, deselect) run through the same event on the same input.
These are three concrete gaps worth knowing about for future work: ship-swapping selection, empty-hex deselect, and split left/right-click intent. Happy to address any of them.

Zero errors. Here's a summary of what was done and found:
Issue 1 — Buttons not clickable (root cause: wrong RectTransform anchors)

The `FrigateButton`, `DestroyerButton`, and `ExplorerButton` `RectTransform`s had `anchorMin/Max = (0.5, 0.5)` (center-center pivot) instead of `(0,0)/(1,1)` (full stretch). A `VerticalLayoutGroup` with `childControlWidth/Height: true` requires stretch anchors to drive child size — with center anchors it can't resize or reposition the buttons, leaving them as 100×100 boxes piled at `(0,0)`and effectively invisible or misplaced at runtime. All three button `RectTransform`s are now set to `Stretch

Issue 2 — BuildPanel covers End Turn button
`BuildPanel` was at `anchoredPosition (0,0)` flush with the bottom-right corner. It's now offset to `(0, 60)` — 60px above the bottom edge, clearing the End Turn button area.
CanBuild diagnostics
`RefreshUI` now calls `EvaluateCanBuild`per ship type, which logs `CanBuild=true/false at (x,y)` each time the panel refreshes. On false, `DiagnoseCanBuild` walks the same guard chain as `ShipBuildManager.CanBuild` and logs the exact first failing reason: null

Clean. Both fill blocks now set the full set of RectTransform properties before touching color:

* `anchorMin = (0, 0)` + `anchorMax = (percent, 1)` — bar spans the correct horizontal fraction, full height
* `sizeDelta = (0, 0)` — no pixel offset fighting the anchors

* `anchoredPosition = (0, 0)` — bar is flush to its parent's bottom-left corner
`RefreshHUD` will apply the same logic on every update, so HP/shield changes during combat will resize the bars correctly too.

Clean compile. A few notes on what to watch for in the logs when you enter Play mode:

* `parentRect=(0,0,0,0)` — the background container (`HPBarBackground` / `ShieldBarBackground`) has zero size. This means the layout group on the row itself hasn't given that container any height yet, and the fill's stretch anchors resolve to nothing. The fix in that case would be adding a `LayoutElement` with an explicit `preferredHeight` to the background containers in the prefab.

* `rect=(0,0,0,0)` but `parentRect` is non-zero — the anchors and offsets are being applied correctly but the canvas hasn't done a layout pass yet. You can force one with `Canvas.ForceUpdateCanvases()`before calling `SetData`, or defer it via `LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(rt)`after.
* `enabled=False` — something upstream is disabling the Image; trace `activeInHierarchy` on the parent chain.

How you return to the galactic map

If you click the button before 3 seconds, both paths race — double LoadSceneAsync calls will be sent. SceneLoader would need to guard against that or it double-loads.
What happens when you get back to the map
Ship data — the UnitManager is DontDestroyOnLoad, so playerShips and enemyShips lists survive. CombatTransition.ReportOutcome() runs before scene transition (called synchronously in CheckVictoryConditions) and calls UnitManager.UnregisterShip() for every enemy ship, removing them from both
What stores combat results between scenes
Entirely via static fields and DontDestroyOnLoad singletons — no PlayerPrefs, no ScriptableObjects:

What	How
Ships going into battle	CombatTransition.PendingEncounter (static field) — set by TriggerCombat, cleared by ReportOutcome
Who won	CombatTransition.ReportOutcome() acts on it immediately (still in Battle scene)
Surviving ship data	Stays in UnitManager.playerShips / UnitManager.enemyShips (DontDestroyOnLoad)
Turn state	GalacticTurnManager (DontDestroyOnLoad)
The one structural gap worth knowing about

UnitManager.hexGrid and shipVisuals are not reset when returning to GameMap. BattleSceneSetup overwrites hexGrid with the battle grid on entry, but nothing restores it to the GameMap grid on exit. Any code in GameMap that calls UnitManager.Instance.SpawnShip or tries to read visual positions will either error (destroyed battle hex grid reference) or silently produce wrong world positions until the GameMap re-assigns the reference somehow. Key Patterns to Reuse

Implementation Notes

* `Start()` is only called once for DontDestroyOnLoad singletons. On return to GameMap, `UnitManager.Start()` does not re-fire — that is why the hex grid goes stale. `MapSceneInitializer` exists specifically to compensate for this.

* `GameSetup.SpawnFleets()` calls `UnitManager.SpawnShip()` which creates visuals via the normal path. On first load, `RebuildAllVisuals()` runs with zero ships and is a no-op, so there is no conflict.

* On first load vs return: `UnitManager.allShips` is empty on first load (GameSetup hasn't run yet when `MapSceneInitializer.Start()` fires, since both wait one frame). This means `RebuildAllVisuals()` will iterate nothing on first load and is safe. Ships created by `GameSetup` one frame later go through `SpawnShip()` which already creates their visuals correctly.
* `HandPannel` CanvasGroup was confirmed present in the Battle scene from the earlier inspection. `CombatResultUI.handPanelGroup` must be wired to it in the Inspector.

* `victoryChecker` field removal — the `CombatResultUI` Inspector currently has `victoryChecker` wired to `/VictoryConditionChecker`. Removing the field from the script will cause Unity to silently drop the serialized value on next compile — no manual scene cleanup needed.

* Double scene load race condition is fully resolved by removing `ReturnToGameMapAfterDelay` — only `OnReturnClicked` can now trigger `LoadSceneAsync("GameMap")`.
* Card interaction blocking uses `CanvasGroup.interactable = false` on the `HandPannel` — this cascades to all children including `CardUI` components without needing to touch each card slot individually.

1. `allShips` contains both teams.
`UnitManager` has three parallel collections:

```
private Dictionary<string, ShipInstance> allShips     // keyed by instanceID — ALL ships, both teams
public  List<ShipInstance>               playerShips   // subset: player team only
public  List<ShipInstance>               enemyShips    // subset: enemy team only

```

`RegisterShip()` adds every ship to `allShips` andto whichever team list applies. `UnregisterShip()`removes from all three. So iterating `allShips.Values`in `RebuildAllVisuals()` is correct — it covers both teams in one pass. No need to loop `playerShips` and `enemyShips` separately.

2. **Add descriptive text (alt text) to charts and images** — The code library we use to generate PowerPoint files supports adding a text description to any chart or image. For decorative images (background gradients, icons next to labelled fields), we would mark them as decorative so they are skipped. For data charts, we would add a brief description of what the chart shows.
All 8 issues are fixable in code — there is no need to redesign the deck or change the data pipelines. The fixes fall into three types:

| Fix Business Initiative column order | 1 day |
| Fix Key Competitors reading order | 1–1.5 days |
| Fix Technology Horizon cover reading order | 0.25 day |
| Fix phantom blank bullets (8 sites) | 0.5 day |
| Fix breadcrumb position (2 files) | 0.5 day |
| Fix Business Initiatives note position | 0.25 day |

|--------|-----------------|
| Add alt text to all images and charts | 0.5 day |

---

## Recommended Next Steps

1. **Prioritise the 4 Critical issues** — particularly alt text on charts (quick win) and the Key Competitors reading order (most broken user experience).
2. Assign a single engineer to the 5-day remediation sprint; all changes are in two TypeScript files (`combineclientPptSlides.ts` and `generateClientCompetitiveLandscape.ts`) plus a handful of supporting files.
3

3. **Fix a double-newline formatting pattern** — A common text-formatting technique currently in use inserts an empty invisible bullet point between each real bullet point. Replacing the double-newline separator with a single newline removes the phantom bullets with no visual impact.
These changes are all backend code changes in the PPT generation service. No changes are required to the frontend, the data pipeline, or the Azure infrastructure.

---

## Effort Estimate

**Prepared for:** Client Radar Product & Engineering Teams
**Date:** 2026-06-22
**Source:** Automated accessibility audit of a generated Client Intel Pack PowerPoint deck

---

## What We Found

The Client Intel Pack PowerPoint files generated by Client Radar contain **8 accessibility issues** that affect users who rely on screen readers (such as JAWS or NVDA) or Microsoft's built-in Accessibilit

## How Many Issues and How Serious Are They?

- **Breadcrumb navigation** (the small path at the top of each slide, e.g., "Client Overview > Key Facts") appears at the correct position for some slides but at the very end of the reading order for others, meaning it is announced after all the main content instead of before it.

- **The Key Competitors table** is read in entirely the wrong order — all the column subheadings across all three competitor columns are announced together first, then the competitor names and data come after. A screen-reader user would hear "Key Service Providers, Key Sub-Services, Key Engagements, Key Service Providers, Key Sub-Services, Key Engagements, Key Service Providers, Key Sub-Services,

|----------|-------|---------------|
| **Critical** | 4 | Core information is missing or presented in an order that makes the content incomprehensible to a screen-reader user |
| **Serious** | 2 | Content is hard to navigate or partially misleading for AT users |
| **Moderate** | 2 | Minor flow disruptions; the content is still usable but not ideal |

### 2. Charts and Images Have No Descriptions
Charts (bar charts, pie charts), icons, background images, and the client logo have no alternative text. A screen reader simply announces "Image" with no further context. For charts that carry key financial and competitive data — like the revenue bar chart and the revenue distribution pie charts on the Financial Snapshot slide — this means the underlyi

- **Footer text** on data slides (containing "Content contains insights generated by AI" and the copyright notice) does not appear in the reading order at all and is invisible to assistive technology.
## How We Would Fix It

- **The Business Initiatives table** has two problems: (a) the leftmost column — "Business Initiative" — is announced *last*, after all the other columns in the row; and (b) a footnote note is announced in the middle of the table, between the column headers and the data, instead of at the end.

1. **Reorder how shapes are added to slides** — In PowerPoint, the reading order is simply the order in which content is added when the file is built. Fixing the inconsistencies means calling the relevant "add content" functions in the correct sequence. This is a low-risk change that does not affect the visual appearance of the deck at all.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<title>Digital Marketing Knowledge Base — 2025–2026</title>
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Newsreader:opsz,wght@6..72,300;6..72,400;6..72,500;6..72,600&family=IBM+Plex+Sans:wght@300;400;500;600&family=IBM+Plex+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
<style>
  :root {
    --bg: #faf8f4; --bg2: #f2efe8; --bg3: #eae6dc;
    --ink: #1a1a1a; --ink2: #333; --ink3: #555; --muted: #888;
    --accent: #c45d2c; --accent2: #a84b20; --accent-bg: #fdf5f0;
    --blue: #2a6496; --green: #3a7d44; --rule: #d4cfc5;
    --dark: #111; --dark2: #1c1c1c;
  }
  * { margin:0; padding:0; box-sizing:border-box; }
  html { scroll-behavior: smooth; }
  body { background:var(--bg); color:var(--ink); font-family:'IBM Plex Sans',sans-serif; font-weight:300; font-size:14px; line-height:1.7; -webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased; }
  header { background:var(--dark); color:var(--bg); border-bottom:4px solid var(--accent); }
